title: Temporal Entity-History Encodings Instead Of Raw Frequencies
summary: The strongest current code still relies on simple in-sample frequency features for high-cardinality categories.
rationale: The strongest current code still relies on simple in-sample frequency features for high-cardinality categories. CatBoost and target-encoding literature suggest ordered or cross-fit entity histories are much stronger and less leaky for sparse categorical fingerprints like driver, race, compound, and their interactions.
implementation_hint: Build expanding or OOF encodings for stop rate, median tyre-life-at-stop, and hazard residual on keys such as `Driver`, `Race`, `Driver x Compound`, `Race x Compound`, and `Race x Stint`, with year-aware backoff. Compare native-cat CatBoost against LightGBM on these strictly OOF encodings.
expected_effect: Likely the cleanest orthogonal gain if the leaderboard rewards hidden categorical strategy fingerprints not captured by physics proxies.
risk: Easy to overfit same-race rows and rare entities; unseen combinations need strong priors and backoff hierarchy.
